channel.title: ICYMI Law — News-like Link Feed
channel.description: Popular news-like links shared recently by legal-type folks.

item.title: Ex-Southern Water boss charged over alleged plan to manipulate water tests
item.description:
Matthew Wright is accused alongside three others of trying to save the firm millions in penalties.

🔥🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 183 · likes: 447

item.title: Trump’s Plan for Science: More Money for A.I., Less for Universities (Gift Article)
item.description:
Michael Kratsios, President Trump’s science adviser, proposed overhauling how the government funds research. Democrats said Mr. Trump’s actions had weakened science.

🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 187 · likes: 380

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Iran strikes on CIA facilities prompt questions about possible Russian role
item.description:
Iranian drone attacks on CIA facilities in the Gulf have prompted U.S. intelligence analysts to investigate whether Russia assisted by providing targeting information or ​advanced drone technology, sa...

🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 165 · likes: 383

item.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
item.description:
The new analysis from a prominent water campaigner emerged after secret court documents were leaked.

🔥🔥 · reposts: 140 · likes: 305

item.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Went Rogue and Attacked a Digital Library (Gift Article)
item.description:
The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

🔥🔥 · reposts: 103 · likes: 303

item.title: Climate crisis-driven heat ‘sucking soils dry’ as extreme drought hits Europe
item.description:
Analysis shows how heatwaves are making atmosphere thirstier, damaging water supplies, food production and power generation

🔥🔥 · reposts: 113 · likes: 236

item.title: ‘Risking blackouts’? How Great Britain’s grid operator was dragged into a political row
item.description:
Neso denies allegations aired by Tory MP that staff took part in cover-up over decision-making in heatwave

item.title: Trump must divulge financial details about businesses in $10bn BBC lawsuit
item.description:
US judge’s ruling could open up window into first family’s multiple ventures, in drawback of lawsuit on media

🔥 · reposts: 47 · likes: 228

item.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
item.description:
The cost of parking closest to the terminal just for a few minutes has hit £10 at two of the UK's busiest airports.

🔥 · reposts: 68 · likes: 175

item.title: Thames Water lenders offer 'golden share' to head off nationalisation
item.description:
The water supplier's lenders are trying to prevent the company from being taken over by the Burnham government

item.title: OpenAI Sued Over ChatGPT’s ‘Dangerous’ Health Advice
item.description:
The case appears to be the first to argue that a chatbot’s advice harmed someone seeking guidance about a medical condition.

🔥 · reposts: 62 · likes: 141

item.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
item.description:
Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close

item.title: As Parasite Spawned Chaos, Taylor Farms Slow-Walked Its Response
item.description:

item.title: Trump Loses Bid to Block BBC Subpoenas in $10 Billion Lawsuit

item.title: The Pentagon, sinking billions into Iran war, is quickly running short on cash
item.description:
With some funds set to dry up within weeks, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is set to press lawmakers Tuesday to address the increasingly urgent shortfall.

item.title: JP Morgan boss warns of ‘consequences’ if Burnham taxes banks
item.description:
Higher taxes on sector could threaten plan for £3bn headquarters in London, says Jamie Dimon

item.title: How Trump’s Immigration Crackdown Is Hurting Elder Care (Gift Article)
item.description:
Retirement communities already grappling with labor shortages are being forced to terminate caregivers at a time when more Americans are living longer.

🔥🔥 · reposts: 169 · likes: 340

item.title: Thames Water announces hosepipe ban for 10.1m customers
item.description:
The water company said the three heatwaves contributed to the ban.

item.title: Error Led to 400 Noncitizens Voting in New Jersey, Governor Says
item.description:
A 2023 software glitch at the state’s Motor Vehicle Commission led to roughly 6,600 noncitizens being registered to vote during the Murphy administration, Gov. Mikie Sherrill announced.

🔥 · reposts: 95 · likes: 325

item.title: 'I can't afford to turn the oven on': 7.4m households struggling to buy essentials
item.description:
As Andy Burnham promises “breathing space” for households, millions are unable to buy items such as clothing, heating and food.

🔥 · reposts: 81 · likes: 176

item.title: Judge Tosses Justice Department Challenge to Minnesota Immigration Laws (Gift Article)
item.description:
The Trump administration, which flooded Minnesota with immigration agents this year, argued that policies limiting cooperation with ICE were unconstitutional.

🔥🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 341 · likes: 959

item.title: Pentagon Withheld Dozens of U.S. Military Injuries in Iran War
item.description:
Central Command is not required to release information about injured troops, especially when they quickly return to duty, a military official said.

🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 413 · likes: 766

item.title: World shift to clean energy is unstoppable, IEA report says
item.description:
The world is shifting rapidly to clean energy but still investing too much in fossil fuels, the IEA says.

🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 303 · likes: 917

item.title: Armored Cars, Now on the American Road (Gift Article)
item.description:
Drivers are paying for amenities once reserved for banks, law enforcement and the military.

item.title: Serbian President Vucic says he will resign within weeks
item.description:
Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said on Saturday he would resign ​within weeks and the country will hold early presidential and parliamentary elections, following 18 months of anti-government prote...

item.title: The ACLU Is Arming Lawyers to Expose State Surveillance Secrets
item.description:
A new toolkit for attorneys in Massachusetts targets the technologies police use—and conceal—to build criminal cases, from facial recognition to AI-written police reports.

🔥🔥 · reposts: 200 · likes: 542

item.title: Everything Trump’s Election Speech Did Not Prove
item.description:
After years of searching for any scrap of evidence that suggests the 2020 election was stolen, the White House still hasn’t found it.

item.title: Not enough water for UK’s datacentre plans, trade body says
item.description:
Industry says government’s failure to address cooling demands means AI growth plans are ‘fatally flawed’

item.title: On a Colorado farm, solar panels bring shade for plants, power – and cash: in pictures

item.description:

item.title: Wealth tax on UK’s super-rich could raise £10bn a year, Andy Burnham told

item.title: He spotted a sports relic in the thrift store. It’s about to make him rich.
item.description:

item.description:
Labour leader will visit king to accept role on Monday and will start making cabinet appointments that afternoon

item.title: Failed Immigration Cases Leave Chicago Prosecutor’s Office Reeling
item.description:
Prosecutors have fled the U.S. attorney’s office in Chicago, angry about the cases they’ve been told to bring against protesters and immigrants.

item.title: DOGE was supposed to be dead. Its remnants are everywhere.
item.description:
The fallout from Elon Musk’s cost-cutting venture can be found across the government if you know where to look.

item.title: Two US Troops Killed, One Missing After Iran Strikes Neighbors
item.description:

item.description:
The implicated products were marketed under several brands, and had been shipped as recently as Thursday.

item.title: They Were Charged With Assaulting ICE Agents. The Cases Are Crumbling.
item.description:

item.description:

item.title: Reflecting Pool peeling probably caused by application flaws, experts say
item.description:
President Donald Trump has blamed vandals, but a Washington Post analysis finds that the peeling tracks closely with stages of the renovation process.

item.title: The CDC Has a Cyclospora Lab. DOGE Downsized It Last Year
item.description:

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Backlash Failed Tremendously
item.description:
For all the hysteria over “woke” casting and ahistorical choices, Christopher Nolan’s epic is on track to make $200 million globally during its opening weekend.

item.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
item.description:

item.title: Climate Change Contributes to a Smokier World (Gift Article)
item.description:
Higher temperatures and drought conditions are contributing to more intense wildfires and extending the summer fire season in North America.

🔥 · reposts: 217 · likes: 662

item.title: Republicans in North Carolina Try to Reduce Early Voting on Sundays and on Campuses (Gift Article)
item.description:
Voting-rights activists said the changes are a blatant attempt by G.O.P. leaders to make it harder for Black voters and students, who tend to vote for Democrats, to cast ballots this fall.

item.title: Takeaways From Trump’s Speech Claiming Election Vulnerabilities and China Interference
item.description:
Documents released by the Trump administration to support the president’s claims did not back up his most aggressive statements.

item.title: Kalshi Will Offer Sports-Style Betting on Drug Trial Results
item.description:
Kalshi Inc. is expanding in biotechnology by offering wagers on the outcomes of clinical trials and regulatory decisions, giving traders a more direct way to bet on key industry catalysts.

item.title: Intel Officials Predict the Pentagon’s Bill for the Iran War Will Exceed $100 Billion
item.description:
The Trump administration has not disclosed its cost estimates for the Iran war.
